children.name: Project Foundations
children.description: Essential infrastructure, distribution manifests, and template assets defining the application's deployment and bootstrapping ecosystem.

children.name: Documentation & Manifests
children.description: Static configuration and guidance files defining project requirements and quirks.

files.description: The primary user and developer manual, outlining the clipboard-based bridge philosophy. It details technical requirements, key features like 'Prompt Custody', and setup instructions for local development.

A mission-critical quirks manifest designed for AI agents to prevent redundant refactoring of intentional workarounds. It documents architectural anomalies like hybrid DPI coordinate handling and strict whitespace normalization.
The exhaustive list of Python library dependencies required by the logic engine. It ensures environment parity and includes specialized packages for tokenization and secret detection.
A master registry of standard code extensions and their descriptions used for the initial application configuration. It serves as the baseline for the frontend's extension-based filtering system.

children.name: Build & Update Tooling
children.description: Automation scripts and build definitions for compiling and packaging CodeMerger.

files.description: The central development task-runner for Windows, orchestrating everything from virtual environment creation to concurrent frontend/backend execution. It maps simple flags to complex CLI sequences for build, debug, and release workflows.
The PyInstaller configuration defining how the Python engine and bundled assets are packed into a portable executable. It includes specific hooks for Tcl/Tk data and Chromium-based webview platforms.

An Inno Setup script that generates the production Windows installer, handling registry key creation and installation path resolution. It ensures shell execution flags are used to avoid elevation-based webview crashes.

The automated CI/CD pipeline for GitHub releases, triggering builds when new version tags are pushed. It handles multi-stage environment setup for Node.js and Python to compile the final distribution artifact.

A standalone Python script compiled into a small executable to handle the self-update process. It executes a blocking check on the parent process ID to ensure a clean overwrite during software updates.

children.name: Genesis Reference Data
children.description: Guiding data used by the Project Starter to influence AI generation and plan structure.

files.description: A database of strategic prompts used in Step 3 of the Project Starter to validate the quality of AI-generated concepts. It provides the metadata used by the UI's guiding-question carousel.
Implementation-focused questions used to audit the feasibility of the AI-generated implementation roadmap. It ensures critical phases like environment setup and testing points are not omitted.
A foundational template defining the structural expectations for a Project Concept document. It acts as few-shot context for the LLM to ensure consistent formatting across different project types.
The reference roadmap template breaking development into distinct logical phases. It helps the LLM break down complex app goals into incremental, actionable implementation steps.

children.name: Project Template Seeds
children.description: Default files and automation scripts injected into new projects scaffolded by CodeMerger.

files.description: A placeholder documentation file for newly created projects, containing dynamic tokens for tech stack and setup details. It encourages best practices in the user's generated codebase.
A replica of CodeMerger's quirks manifest provided to the user's project to support their own AI-driven development. It establishes a standard for documenting workaround logic from the start.
A semantic versioning anchor for the scaffolded project, enabling immediate use of the included release automation scripts. It standardizes the version tracking format for the user.
A reusable version of CodeMerger's release logic provided to the user. It automates Git tagging and branch validation for their newly generated software.
A boilerplate task runner for Python projects, pre-configured with virtual environment and freeze logic. It provides users with a standardized entry point for running and building their code.
A node-specific task runner script provided during project generation. It encapsulates common npm commands into the unified 'go' interface used throughout the CodeMerger ecosystem.
The default orchestration script for generated projects requiring containerization. It simplifies Docker-Compose interactions for users starting with microservice-oriented stacks.

children.name: Logic Engine
children.description: The Python backend managing file systems, LLM interactions, and multi-window state synchronization.

children.name: Core Orchestration
children.description: Foundational modules handling application entry, global state, and system utilities.

files.description: The main bootstrapper that configures Win32 DPI awareness and registers MIME types for ES modules. It initializes the API bridge and starts the PyWebView event loop.
The central repository for persistent application settings and cross-window coordination. It implements a non-destructive reconciliation strategy to merge configurations from concurrent instances.
Defines global magic numbers, color palettes, and performance thresholds. Crucially, it hosts the marker fragments used to avoid recursive self-parsing errors during code bundling.
Handles OS-specific path resolution for bundled assets and persistent user data. It distinguishes between the immutable install directory and the writable %APPDATA% config root.
Interfaces with the Windows Registry to manage system-level preferences like automatic update toggles. It provides fallbacks between HKCU and HKLM scopes.

A collection of critical logic utilities, including the Named Mutex single-instance detector and the Tiktoken context window calculator. It hosts the atomic file-writing logic used to prevent config corruption.

children.name: File & Data Intelligence
children.description: Services responsible for scanning codebases and managing project-specific configurations.

files.description: The high-level controller for project lifecycle management, responsible for initializing new .allcode profiles and maintaining the in-memory disk inventory. It provides the concurrency locks required to synchronize the monitor thread and user API requests.
The logic wrapper for the .allcode file, handling profile creation, file selection state, and per-file token counting. It includes defensive loading checks to ignore collision-based empty files.

An optimized disk walker that extracts project file lists while simultaneously discovering and parsing .gitignore patterns. It is designed to move expensive IO operations out of the UI rendering loop.
The transformation engine that converts flat file lists into hierarchical Trie data for the frontend. It features an inventory-first mode for instant UI results on codebases with 10k+ files.
A daemon thread that performs background filesystem checks to alert users of new or deleted files. It uses adaptive throttling based on scan duration to minimize the app's CPU footprint.

Configures a sophisticated dual-logging system using the Rich library for console diagnostics and rotating file handlers for persistent logs. It includes dummy streams to prevent crashes in windowed mode.

children.name: LLM Context Operations
children.description: Specialized logic for bundling code, checking security, and applying AI-generated modifications.

files.description: Bundles selected files into the application's unique machine-parseable Markdown format. It manages the injection of mode-specific system instructions like 'Fast Apply' vs 'Full File'.

The prompt engineering hub, containing the large prose blocks that dictate LLM stylistic guidelines and project generation constraints. It keeps logic and instruction text separate for easier maintenance.
A security auditing service that uses high-entropy detection to find API keys and credentials in code selections. It provides the metadata for the pre-copy warning dialogs.

A sophisticated fuzzy matching engine capable of applying code patches even when line numbers or indentation vary from the model's reference. It uses multi-strategy matching to ensure reliable surgical updates.
The top-level parser for LLM responses, extracting file blocks and XML tags from Markdown. It coordinates the actual filesystem writes and standardizes all content to LF to prevent phantom diffs.
Leverages the Pygments library to generate the syntax-highlighted HTML used in the response review window. It calculates line-level differences using the standard library sequence matcher.

children.name: GUI Management
children.description: Handles the lifecycle of multiple UI windows and high-DPI coordination.

files.description: The central UI coordinator managing transitions between splash, main, and compact modes. It handles the 'handshake' logic that waits for frontend readiness before revealing the dashboard.
Provides the mathematical foundation for Windows High-DPI coordination. It ensures windows are sized in logical units but positioned in physical units to avoid coordinate drift on scaled monitors.
Constructs the animated frameless splash screen. it uses inline Base64 assets and CSS keyframes to provide immediate visual feedback while the engine initializes in the background.
Initializes the primary dashboard window and binds backend state callbacks to window events. It uses local HTTP serving to satisfy CORS requirements for ES modules.
Creates the minimalist companion widget. it includes specialized Win32 API calls to hide the widget from the taskbar, anchoring the app presence to the minimized main window.
Orchestrates the software update lifecycle, checking for releases and launching the external GUI installer. It uses forceful termination to ensure file locks are released for the updater process.

children.name: API Bridge Partials
children.description: Modular implementations of the frontend-accessible bridge methods.

children.name: Core Logic Bridges
children.description: Bridge parts for basic system, configuration, and project-level operations.

files.description: The master bridge class combining all partial mixins into a single interface. It is the only object explicitly exposed to the JavaScript context for method calls.
The foundational mixin providing shared helper methods, JSON response formatters, and thread-safe dialog triggers used across all specialized API parts.
Exposes low-level window controls to the frontend, including programmatic resizing, coordinate sync for drag logic, and asset encoding.
Manages global application settings and provides the bridge for clipboard utilities like the comment-cleanup prompt and update checking.
Handles directory-level interactions, including loading projects, switching profiles, and opening folder-level command prompts or file explorers.

children.name: Feature Bridge Modules
children.description: Bridge parts supporting the main workspace features like file management and review.

files.description: The interface for the merge list, providing tree data and token estimates. it also generates the architectural context prompts for the Project Visualizer.
Bypasses browser security to provide direct system clipboard access. it handles remote paste requests used to bridge the compact widget and main window review.
The backend controller for the AI Response Review workflow. it routes Markdown parsing and handles the execution of individual file approvals or deletions.
The aggregator for all Project Starter logic. it manages session initialization and restoration when entering and exiting the project genesis workflow.
Handles the storage and retrieval of project starter drafts from persistent JSON. it also manages the export/import of project genesis configuration files.

children.name: Starter Process Logic
children.description: Specialized sub-APIs for the multi-step Project Starter wizard.

files.description: Implements the segmented parsing logic for Project Starter AI outputs. it identifies specific XML sections to populate individual concept and TODO drafts.
Constructs the complex, high-context prompts required to guide an LLM through architectural concept and boilerplate generation.
The final implementation bridge of the Project Starter. it handles directory creation, boilerplate writing, and project initialization based on the final AI output.

children.name: User Interface
children.description: The Vue 3 frontend providing interactive dashboards, real-time code management, and architectural mapping.

children.name: Framework & Core Config
children.description: The foundational environment and styling definitions for the web application.

files.description: The Node.js manifest defining frontend dependencies like Vue 3 and Vite. it includes scripts for concurrent backend/frontend development.
Configures the Vite build system, ensuring all assets use relative paths for compatibility with the webview local filesystem protocol.
Defines the design tokens and color scheme. it maps specialized theme keys to the CodeMerger dark-mode palette for consistent UI styling.
A standard configuration for processing CSS through PostCSS, ensuring Tailwind directives and autoprefixer work correctly during production builds.
The primary entry page for the web engine. it provides the single DOM mount point for the Vue application and the favicon declaration.
The JavaScript entry point, responsible for initializing the Vue app, registering the router, and applying the custom info-mode directive.
The global CSS manifest containing Tailwind layers and custom reset logic. It defines common animations like the 'click-pulse' and loading dot sequences.
Configures the frontend routes using hash-based history. it manages the navigation between the main dashboard and the specialized compact widget view.

children.name: Global State Handlers
children.description: Reactivity modules managing the bridge data and application-level business logic.

files.description: The central repository for reactive singleton state. it stores active project metadata, shared image assets, and session-only review progress.
The high-level orchestrator for the frontend, aggregating all other hooks. it manages the application initialization sequence and bridge event listeners.
Controls the interactive documentation state. it manages the stack of active hover keys and resolves them into descriptive help text for the footer.
Provides system-level actions like programmatic window resizing, config saving, and management of global application preferences.
Exposes hooks for project-specific operations, including directory selection, profile management, and the 'add-all' new file logic.
Manages the logic for the AI Response Review system, coordinating clipboard pasting and individual file modification approvals.
A specialized thin client for the Project Starter API, exposing session management and segmented generation hooks to the UI wizard.
A utility hook for consistent keyboard navigation. it binds escape key events to modal closures across different workspace components.
Implements the complex logic for toggling between Markdown previews and raw editors while maintaining stable scroll positions for the user.
A specialized handler for the compact widget's unique constraints, managing title abbreviations and the minimalist feedback overlay system.

children.name: Visual Documentation
children.description: Utility modules and dictionaries supporting the 'Info Mode' help system.

files.description: A custom Vue directive that captures mouse hover events and pushes associated documentation keys to the global state for display in the Info Panel.
The main aggregator for the Info Mode system, merging specialized dictionaries from different functional modules into a single searchable object.
Contains documentation strings for the primary dashboard and project selector, explaining basic navigation and workspace interactions.
Provides detailed interaction guidance for the merge list editor, including filter behavior and file reordering controls.
Houses help text for the AI Response Review window, describing tab contents and individual file approval workflows.
Contains descriptions for every toggle and input in the global settings menu, clarifying performance and security impacts.
Detailed help strings for the project genesis tool, guiding users through concept, stack, and TODO generation steps.

children.name: Main UI Layouts
children.description: The primary views and headers representing the application's root containers.

files.description: The root component coordinating the global layout. It hosts the loading overlay, status bar, and Info Panel while globally intercepting shortcuts and paste events.
The primary dashboard view. it manages the lifecycle of top-level modals and provides the logic for handling remote review signals from the compact widget.
A specialized minimalist view for the companion widget. it implements a custom mouse-drag engine for window positioning and always-on-top interactions.
The top-level navigation element for the dashboard, managing project renaming and the masked project swatch visual styling.
Hosts the workspace control row, providing the profile switcher and entry points for the Merge List editor and Project Visualizer.
The central action card of the main window, providing primary buttons for context copying and AI response importing.

children.name: Workspace Modules
children.description: Interactive tools for context management and architectural visualization.

files.description: The container for the merge list editor. It orchestrates the dual-panel layout and handles search sequencing to prevent asynchronous UI race conditions.
Manages the available project files tree. It includes smart logic for range-selection and the batch addition of files based on current filtering.
Controls the merge order list and reordering toolbar. It provides the interface for 'Order Requests' to optimize how the AI reads project context.
A recursive component representing the project hierarchy. it implements visual completeness logic to color folder icons based on their selection status.
An interactive architectural map of the project. it uses a 2D map algorithm to group files into semantic layers based on AI categorization JSON.

children.name: AI Review System
children.description: Tools for inspecting, diffing, and applying AI-generated code modifications.

files.description: The primary interface for AI response evaluation. It categorizes output into distinct tabs and manages the batch-application of proposed codebase changes.
Renders line-by-line file comparisons using syntax-highlighted HTML from the backend engine. it handles scrolling and segment anchoring for large source files.
A stylized wrapper for AI commentary. it includes custom pre-processors for rendering interactive TODO checkboxes and high-contrast code blocks.
A specialized tab for managing interactive file diffs. it organizes changes by type and provides global controls to collapse or expand modification blocks.
Displays testing steps provided by the AI. it includes session-based history navigation to allow users to revisit previous verification instructions.
Represents an individual file modification in the review list. it provides granular control over individual acceptance, rejection, and undo actions.
A collapsible section for the AI's technical explanation of changes. it allows users to read architectural context without leaving the diff workflow.
A diagnostic overlay triggered when AI output lacks proper formatting. It provides a specialized correction prompt based on the specific parser failure.
Handles validation failures during file reordering. it identifies missing or duplicate files in the AI response to help the user request a correction.

children.name: Settings & Overlays
children.description: Configuration dialogs and shared global utility components.

children.name: Settings Workspace
children.description: Tabs and modules for application-wide configuration.

files.description: The master container for application preferences. it uses a sidebar-driven tab system to organize system, file manager, and editor settings.
Manages core application behaviors like monitor intervals, secret scanning toggles, and automatic update preferences.
Configures the merge list editor, including token thresholds and 'Add All' safety limits to prevent context overflow.
An interactive registry for managing code extensions. it allows users to define custom filetypes and toggle active indexing status for defaults.
Provides editors for global instruction prompts, allowing users to define a baseline code standard for all AI interactions.
Configuration for the Project Starter, focusing on the default parent directory for new codebase generation.
Configures the code editor integration, allowing users to select their preferred executable for opening files directly from the UI.

children.name: Global Utilities
children.description: Shared components providing application-wide services and overlays.

files.description: The dynamic documentation footer. it serves as the visual output for the v-info system, displaying context-aware tips based on user hover state.
A focused browser for recent and new projects, featuring real-time path filtering and masked logo swatches.
A project-specific interface for defining Intro and Outro prompts. it allows users to wrap merged context in custom architectural goals.
A dialog for creating isolated configuration sets within a project, supporting the cloning of existing merge lists and instructions.
A notification layer alerting the user to new default extensions introduced by software updates, ensuring the file manager stays current.
A pixel-driven HSL wheel for picking project accent colors. it includes contrast calculation logic to ensure readable UI labels.
A shared utility library for color mathematics. it provides functions for determining relative luminance and contrast-optimized text modes.

children.name: Project Starter Workflow
children.description: The multi-step wizard for project concept generation and boilerplate scaffolding.

children.name: Genesis Core
children.description: The primary layout and logic steps for creating new codebases.

files.description: The master orchestrator for the project creation wizard. it manages navigation across the six generation phases and tracks global session state.
Collects basic project metadata and manages the selection of existing projects as references for 'v2' rewrite scenarios.
A specialized tree-manager for selecting reference source code during the generation of a new project's concept.
Manages the interactive loop for generating and refining project features and principles. it handles the transition between segmented and merged document views.
The tech-stack selection module. it allows users to review AI technology recommendations and audit the architectural warnings for each choice.
Coordinates the generation of phase-by-phase implementation roadmaps, ensuring they align with the generated concept and tech stack.
The final stage of project genesis, validating the final AI response and requesting the backend to scaffold the project files to disk.
The landing screen shown after successful project creation. it provides immediate links to open the new folder and activate the project profile.

children.name: Starter UI Helpers
children.description: Widgets and overlays supporting the iterative refinement of project plans.

files.description: A Markdown reviewer for merged documents. it supports context-aware AI questioning and triggers instructional rewriting for the whole file.
A specialized interface for multi-section AI outputs. it allows users to lock finalized segments while requesting rewrites for specific incomplete drafts.
An interactive panel that cycles through quality-assurance criteria for project drafts. it provides 'Copy context' prompts needed for AI refinement loops.
A draggable overlay for providing high-level instructions to modify project segments. it manages the assembly of locked and unsigned context for the AI.
A simple viewer for AI-provided change summaries. it helps users understand exactly what was modified during an instructional rewrite session.

children.name: Compact Mode Components
children.description: Specialized UI elements for the minimalist companion widget.

files.description: A custom notification layer for the widget, providing success/error banners and specialized interfaces for binary confirmations like memory overwrites.
The standard button set for the compact mode dashboard, providing primary controls for context copying and Importing changes.
A minimalist, single-character interaction layer designed for maximum space efficiency when the Ultra Compact UI mode is active.
